intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

body.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.title: recipe app for people who hate cooking
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer gentle, approachable UI that feels less intimidating and more forgiving
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Design should use generous border-radius, smooth corners on cards/buttons; minimize angular/geometric severity
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer cleaner, more structured geometry; sharp corners and crisp edges could feel more efficient and less 'childish'

evidence.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer gentle, approachable UI that feels less intimidating and more forgiving
evidence.implication: Design should use generous border-radius, smooth corners on cards/buttons; minimize angular/geometric severity

facade.hypothesis: Users of recipe-averse cooks prefer frictionless, zero-delay ingredient capture over animated onboarding or gesture-heavy interactions
facade.label: Instant ingredient snap
facade.acceptImplies: The app should minimize animation and transitions in favor of immediate, tap-based actions for core tasks like adding ingredients or starting timers
facade.rejectImplies: Users are willing to tolerate or prefer animated transitions, gesture-based controls, or multi-step interactions if they feel more intuitive or satisfying

facade.hypothesis: For a recipe app targeting cooking-averse users, a soft, approachable visual language (warm neutrals, rounded corners, gentle shadows) reduces friction and makes the experience feel less clinical or intimidating than stark white interfaces.
facade.label: Warm beige cards
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward comfort and approachability over minimalist or high-contrast design. Soft, inviting aesthetics build trust in a tool for non-cooks.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clarity, modernity, or visual punch. May want the app to feel efficient and no-nonsense rather than cozy—appeals to users who want speed over warmth.

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer a persistent sidebar showing all recipes at once over tab-based navigation, reducing clicks to find what they want
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation pattern: sidebar persistence vs tab switching
facade.label: Sidebar with recipe list
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick scanning and always-visible recipe options; likely prefers dense, reference-heavy layouts where everything is accessible without mode-switching
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers focused views; likely wants tabs or modal-based navigation that hides options until needed; may value clarity over quick-access density

facade.hypothesis: For people who hate cooking, stripping away narrative and showing only essential data (ingredient names, quantities, units) reduces cognitive load and decision fatigue at the start of the cooking journey.
facade.axisTargeted: narrative density — recipe storytelling and chef voice vs. pure functional data
facade.label: Just the ingredients list
facade.acceptImplies: Users want minimalist, non-decorative recipe formats. Likely to favor: metric-first design, ingredient checkboxes, skip-the-story flows, maybe even ingredient substitution tables over descriptive text.
facade.rejectImplies: Users may need some narrative scaffolding (why this ingredient, what it does) to feel confident. Likely to favor: conversational recipe intros, ingredient explanations, visual confidence-builders, reassurance copy that non-cooks respond to.

facade.hypothesis: A conversational, forgiving tone ('Ugh, same' rather than 'Prepare ingredients') will feel less intimidating and more like a friend who gets it than a neutral instruction manual
facade.label: Lazy voice
facade.acceptImplies: User resonates with casual, commiserating language — embrace humor and permission-giving in copy and UI. 'Why cook when this exists?' not 'Here's how to prepare a meal.'
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clarity and respect over buddy-buddy tone — lean into clean, straightforward language that treats them as capable but busy, not as someone who needs hand-holding.

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
Whether user wants minimal decision-making (curated picks) vs. maximum filtering control (dietary, time, skill filters)
Whether user prefers instant gratification (no loading, snappy) vs. exploratory browsing with smooth transitions and discovery

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants minimal decision-making (curated picks) vs. maximum filtering control (dietary, time, skill filters)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers instant gratification (no loading, snappy) vs. exploratory browsing with smooth transitions and discovery
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users intimidated by cooking respond better to softer, warmer light treatment that feels cozy rather than clinical or sterile
facade.label: Warm, dim lighting
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace warm color palettes (ambers, golds, warm grays) and lower contrast designs; avoid bright whites and high-contrast clinical aesthetics; lighting metaphors in imagery
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward brighter, cooler lighting; higher contrast; more clinical/modern aesthetic; potentially cleaner, more minimal look
